title: Spacing between inline elements should be explicit
description: Spacing between inline elements should be explicitAnalyze your codeconsistency-formattedmaintainabilityreliabilityCode SmellreactWhy is this an issue?More InfoReact JSX differs from the HTML standard in the way it handles newline characters and surrounding whitespace. HTML collapses multiple whitespace characters (including newlines) into a single whitespace, but JSX removes such sequences completely, leaving no space between inline elements separated by the line break. This difference in behavior can be confusing and may result in unintended layout, for example, missing whitespace between the link content and the surrounding text.To avoid such issues, you should never rely on newline characters in JSX, and explicitly specify whether you want whitespace between inline elements separated by
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

noncompliant:
{/* Noncompliant: ambiguous spacing */} Here is some space
{/* Noncompliant: ambiguous spacing */} No space between these

checks.message: Use explicit spacing {' '} between inline elements instead of relying on newlines
Add explicit spacing {' '} between text and inline elements
Add explicit spacing {' '} after inline elements before text
Use explicit spacing {' '} between consecutive inline elements
